text: U.S. government research into the health consequences of nuclear radiation involved systematic violations of the human rights of the Marshallese people. Evidence suggests that radioactive fallout from Pacific weapons testing was likely deposited across a much broader range of Micronesian islands than officially acknowledged. To rectify this history of opacity, the U.S. government must declassify and release all data regarding testing protocols, fallout patterns, and the subsequent health impacts on the populations of the Marshall Islands and greater Micronesia. Furthermore, the medical community has a professional and ethical responsibility to hold nuclear-armed states accountable to their treaty obligations regarding the total elimination of these weapons. Finally, as an urgent matter of social justice and to uphold the fundamental human right to health, the federal government should restore Medicaid eligibility for all Micronesian migrants living in the U.S. under the Compact of Free Association.
